titleStmt.title: SII 9.569: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 569. (A.R. No. 527 of 1915.) ON A STONE SET UP AT THE VILLAGE-GATE AT BHATTARABOMMALAPURAM, ADONI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is dated Śaka 1457, Jaya, Pushya, śu. 3, Sōmavāra, Saṅkramaṇa, corresponding to A.D. 1534 December 7, Monday, which was not, however, a day of any saṅkramaṇa. It records that king Achyutadēva-Mahārāya, who was ruling at Vidyānagara, made a gift of the village Bommalāpura on the bank of the Tungabhadrā and situated in the Sātanūru-sīme, a division of Ādavāni-rājya, to Bhaṭara Nāgaya, son of Kaḍabūra Reḍyapaya of the Bhaṭ-ānvaya.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
namastuṁgaśiraścuṁbicaṁdracāmaracāra
vē . trailōkyanagarāraṁbhamūlastaṁbhāya śaṁ
bhavē . svasti samastapṛthvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara vīrapratāpa
śrīvīraAccutadēvamahārāyaru vidyānagarada nelavīḍinoḷu sukhasaṁkathāvinō
dadiṁda pruthvīrājyaṁgaiuttamirddu vartamāna śālivāhanaśakavarṣaṁgaḷu 1457 ne
ya jayasaṁvatsarada puṣya śuddha 3 sōmavārada saṁkramaṇapu(ṁ)ṇyakāladalu śrīma
dbhaṭānvaya kaḍabūra reḍyapayana maga bhaṭara nāgayage koṭa dharma . Ā
davāniya rājyake saluva sātanūra sīmeyoḷagaṇa tuṁgabhadrātīrada boṁ
malāpuraveṁba grāmavanu ninage sarvvamānya Agrahāravāgi sahira(ṁ)ṇyōdakadā
nadhārāpūrvaka nīradhāreyaneṟadu koṭevāgi Ā grāmadiṁda huṭida
nidhinikṣēpa jala pāṣāṇa Akṣīṇi Āgāmi sidha sādhyaṁgaḷeṁba Aṣṭa
bhōga tēja svāmya hadineṁṭumaha sakalōtpatti sakala suvarnādā
ya sakala dhānyada kūranū nīu niṁma putrapautrapāraṁpara
Ācaṁdrārkkasthāyigaḷāgi sukhadali Anubhavisuvadū
yeṁdu māḍida dharma �yo dharmake Āru tapidare Avaru
taṁma mātā pitrugaḷa kāśiyalu koṁda dōśakke hōharu
ślō . dānapālanayōrmadhyē dānā(ṁ)tsēyōnrupālanaṁ .
dānasvargamavāpnōti pālanādacutaṁ padhaṁ . svadattādvigu
ṇaṁ pu(ṁ)ṇyaṁ paradattānupālanaṁ . paradattāapahārēṇa sva
dattaṁ niṣphalaṁ bhavētu .
Digital edition of SII 9.569 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
